sections.title: 1. Overview
sections.content: The Constant Contact connector syncs contacts, email campaigns, and contact lists from a Constant Contact account into the Shielva knowledge base. It uses the Constant Contact v3 API with OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow. Resources are normalized into ConnectorDocument objects with stable, SHA-256 content-addressable IDs. The connector supports cursor-based pagination for all list endpoints, per-item error isolation during sync, and exponential-backoff retry on network and rate-limit errors.

children.title: 1.1 What This Connector Does
children.content:
The `ConstantContactConnector` class connects to the Constant Contact v3 API via OAuth 2.0 to read contacts, email campaigns, and contact lists from the user's Constant Contact account. It performs cursor-based paginated fetches starting from each resource endpoint.

Each resource is normalized into a `ConnectorDocument` and can be ingested into the Shielva knowledge base. Features include:
- Full cursor-based pagination via `_links.next.href` cursor extraction
- Per-item error isolation (individual normalization failures are counted without aborting the batch)
- Exponential-backoff retry on 429 and network errors via `with_retry()`
- Multi-tenant ID namespacing via SHA-256 stable IDs (`SHA-256('contact:{contact_id}')[:16]`)
- Zero retry on auth errors — these require human intervention

children.title: 1.2 Connector Identity
children.content:
| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Provider | Constant Contact |
| Service | Constant Contact |
| Connector Class | `ConstantContactConnector` |
| Auth Type | OAuth 2.0 (`oauth2`) |
| Sync Resources | Contacts, Email Campaigns |
| Current Version | 1.0.0 |
| `CONNECTOR_TYPE` | `"constant_contact"` |
| `CONNECTOR_NAME` | `"Constant Contact"` |
| `AUTH_URL` | `"https://authz.constantcontact.com/oauth2/default/v1/authorize"` |
| `TOKEN_URL` | `"https://authz.constantcontact.com/oauth2/default/v1/token"` |
| `SCOPES` | `"contact_data campaign_data account_read"` |

sections.title: 2. Authentication
sections.content: Authentication uses OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow. The connector requires a `client_id` and `client_secret` from a Constant Contact Developer application.

children.title: 2.1 OAuth Flow
children.content:
The OAuth flow issues an access token and refresh token with the requested scopes. The `authorize()` method returns the authorization URL string. After the user authorizes, Constant Contact redirects with a code that is exchanged for tokens.

- Required scopes: `contact_data`, `campaign_data`, `account_read`
- Auth URI: `https://authz.constantcontact.com/oauth2/default/v1/authorize`
- Token URI: `https://authz.constantcontact.com/oauth2/default/v1/token`

Tokens are stored in the connector config as `access_token`, `refresh_token`, and `token_expires_at`.

children.title: 2.2 Install Fields
children.content:
| Field | Required | Type | Description |
|-------|----------|------|-------------|
| `client_id` | Yes | string | Constant Contact API Key (Client ID) from the Developer Portal |
| `client_secret` | Yes | password | Constant Contact Client Secret from the Developer Portal |
| `redirect_uri` | No | string | Must match the redirect URI registered in your Constant Contact application |

sections.title: 3. Connector Methods
sections.content: The connector exposes the following methods:

children.title: 3.1 install()
children.content: Validates that `client_id` and `client_secret` are present. Does not make any API calls. Returns `InstallResult` with `AuthStatus.PENDING` on success, directing the user to complete OAuth authorization. Returns `AuthStatus.MISSING_CREDENTIALS` with `ConnectorHealth.OFFLINE` if either credential is absent.

children.title: 3.2 authorize()
children.content: Returns the Constant Contact OAuth2 authorization URL as a string. The URL includes `client_id`, `response_type=code`, and the scopes `contact_data campaign_data account_read`. Optionally includes `redirect_uri` and `state` parameters.

children.title: 3.3 health_check()
children.content:
Calls `GET /v3/account/summary` to verify connectivity and token validity. Returns `HealthCheckResult` with:
- `HEALTHY` + `CONNECTED` when the API responds successfully; populates `organization_name`
- `DEGRADED` + `TOKEN_EXPIRED` on 401/403
- `DEGRADED` + `FAILED` on network errors or other connector errors
- `OFFLINE` + `MISSING_CREDENTIALS` when no access token is present

children.title: 3.4 sync()
children.content:
Performs a full sync of contacts and email campaigns using cursor-based pagination. Each resource is normalized and counted. Returns `SyncResult` with:
- `COMPLETED` when all resources synced without failures
- `PARTIAL` when some resources failed normalization
- `FAILED` when a fatal exception (e.g., network error) aborts the sync

The sync engine fetches all pages by following `_links.next.href` cursor values.

children.title: 3.5 list_contacts()
children.content: Returns a page of contacts as `List[ConnectorDocument]`. Accepts optional `cursor` for pagination and `limit` (default 500). Each document has `type='contact'` and a stable ID derived from `SHA-256('contact:{contact_id}')[:16]`.

children.title: 3.6 list_campaigns()
children.content: Returns a page of email campaigns as `List[ConnectorDocument]`. Accepts optional `cursor` for pagination. Each document has `type='email_campaign'` and a stable ID derived from `SHA-256('campaign:{campaign_id}')[:16]`.

children.title: 3.7 list_contact_lists()
children.content: Returns a page of contact lists as `List[ConnectorDocument]`. Accepts optional `cursor` for pagination. Each document has `type='contact_list'` and a stable ID derived from `SHA-256('list:{list_id}')[:16]`.

children.title: 3.8 get_contact(contact_id)
children.content: Fetches and normalizes a single contact by its `contact_id`. Returns a `ConnectorDocument` with `type='contact'`.

sections.title: 4. Normalizers
sections.content: All normalization functions live in `helpers/utils.py`. They take raw API response dicts and return `ConnectorDocument` objects with stable, SHA-256 content-addressable IDs.

children.title: 4.1 normalize_contact(c, connector_id, tenant_id)
children.content:
Converts a Constant Contact contact API dict to a `ConnectorDocument`.

- `id` = `SHA-256('contact:{contact_id}')[:16]`
- `type` = `'contact'`
- `title` = `'{first_name} {last_name}'`
- `content` = human-readable text (name, email, phone, company, job title, timestamps)
- `metadata` includes: `contact_id`, `email`, `phone`, `company_name`, `job_title`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `list_memberships`

children.title: 4.2 normalize_campaign(c, connector_id, tenant_id)
children.content:
Converts a Constant Contact email campaign API dict to a `ConnectorDocument`.

- `id` = `SHA-256('campaign:{campaign_id}')[:16]`
- `type` = `'email_campaign'`
- `title` = campaign name
- `content` = human-readable text (name, status, type, timestamps)
- `metadata` includes: `campaign_id`, `name`, `status`, `campaign_type`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `campaign_activities`

children.title: 4.3 normalize_list(l, connector_id, tenant_id)
children.content:
Converts a Constant Contact contact list API dict to a `ConnectorDocument`.

- `id` = `SHA-256('list:{list_id}')[:16]`
- `type` = `'contact_list'`
- `title` = list name
- `content` = human-readable text (name, description, status, member count, timestamps)
- `metadata` includes: `list_id`, `name`, `description`, `status`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `membership_count`

sections.title: 5. HTTP Client
sections.content: The `ConstantContactHTTPClient` in `client/http_client.py` handles all raw API calls. It uses `aiohttp` and accepts Bearer tokens. All endpoints return raw parsed JSON dicts.

children.title: 5.1 Endpoints
children.content:
| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `get_account_info()` | `GET /v3/account/summary` | Account details for health check |
| `get_contacts(cursor, limit)` | `GET /v3/contacts` | Contacts with cursor pagination |
| `get_contact(contact_id)` | `GET /v3/contacts/{contact_id}` | Single contact |
| `get_contact_lists(cursor)` | `GET /v3/contact_lists` | Contact lists with cursor pagination |
| `get_email_campaigns(cursor)` | `GET /v3/emails` | Email campaigns with cursor pagination |
| `get_campaign_activity(activity_id)` | `GET /v3/emails/activities/{id}` | Single campaign activity |
| `get_campaign_reports(cursor)` | `GET /v3/reports/email_reports/campaign_sends` | Campaign send reports |
| `refresh_token()` | `POST token_url` | Refresh the OAuth access token |

children.title: 5.2 Cursor Extraction
children.content:
Constant Contact uses cursor-based pagination. The next page cursor is embedded in `_links.next.href` as the `cursor` query parameter.

```python
links = {"next": {"href": "https://api.cc.email/v3/contacts?cursor=abc123&limit=500"}}
cursor = ConstantContactHTTPClient._extract_cursor(links)
# cursor == "abc123"
```

The `_extract_cursor()` static method parses this URL and returns the cursor string, or `None` when there are no more pages.

children.title: 5.3 Error Mapping
children.content:
| HTTP Status | Exception |
|-------------|----------|
| 401, 403 | `ConstantContactAuthError` |
| 404 | `ConstantContactNotFoundError` |
| 429 | `ConstantContactRateLimitError` (with `retry_after`) |
| 5xx | `ConstantContactError` (server error) |
| Network error | `ConstantContactNetworkError` |

sections.title: 6. Retry Logic
sections.content:
The `with_retry()` helper in `helpers/utils.py` implements exponential backoff with jitter.

- Max attempts: 3
- Base delay: 1.0 s
- Backoff factor: 2.0 (1s → 2s → 4s)
- Jitter: up to 0.5 s random
- Max delay: 30 s
- Auth errors (`ConstantContactAuthError`) are NOT retried — they require human action
- Rate limit errors respect the `Retry-After` header when present

sections.title: 7. Exception Hierarchy
sections.content:
```
ConstantContactError (base)
├── ConstantContactAuthError        (401/403 — not retried)
├── ConstantContactNetworkError     (timeouts, connection failures — retried)
├── ConstantContactNotFoundError    (404 — not retried)
└── ConstantContactRateLimitError   (429 — retried with Retry-After)
```

All exceptions carry `.message`, `.status_code`, and `.code` attributes. `ConstantContactRateLimitError` additionally carries `.retry_after`.

sections.title: 8. Test Suite
sections.content:
The test suite at `tests/test_connector.py` contains 123 tests. All tests use `unittest.mock.AsyncMock` — zero real network I/O.

Run with:
```bash
cd /path/to/constant_contact_connector
python -m pytest tests/ -v
```

Test categories:
- Exception classes (8 tests)
- Models (7 tests)
- Normalizers (22 tests)
- with_retry (6 tests)
- HTTP client cursor extraction (5 tests)
- HTTP client auth headers (3 tests)
- HTTP client _raise_for_status (6 tests)
- HTTP client endpoints (10 tests)
- Connector attributes (8 tests)
- install() (5 tests)
- authorize() (7 tests)
- health_check() (6 tests)
- sync() (7 tests)
- list_contacts() (4 tests)
- list_campaigns() (3 tests)
- list_contact_lists() (3 tests)
- get_contact() (3 tests)
- Lifecycle (4 tests)
- Multi-tenant isolation (3 tests)
